Japanese: 同日　某時刻 高菱屋　大展示場
English: October 13 Lordly Tailor Main Exhibition Hall

Japanese: あたしたちの秘宝展‥‥ もう、ダメになっちゃうのかな。
English: The Treasures of Kurain exhibit is all ruined now...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: Maya...

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっぴり、ザンネンだな。
English: I'm sorry, it's just so sad...

Japanese: 霊媒のコト‥‥みんなに知って もらう、チャンスだったのに。
English: This was our big chance for everyone to learn about spirit channeling...

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと、 元気づけてあげるか）
English: (Maybe I can cheer her up somehow...)

Japanese: まあ、ツボも戻ったことだし、 再開できるんじゃないかな。
English: Well, now that we've got the Sacred Urn back, maybe they can reopen it?

Japanese: そ。そうかな‥‥
English: R-Really...?

Japanese: 《怪人もほしがった、あのツボ！》 ‥‥って書いておけば、
English: Sure! Maybe we can label it, "The Urn of Mask☆DeMasque's Desires"!

Japanese: かえって、お客が いっぱい入るかもしれないし。
English: That would probably attract a lot of attention!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥うお。うおおおおおおお！ それって、いわゆるなるほどくん！
English: Whoa. Whoa. Whoa!! That's brilliant, Nick!

Japanese: “大もうけ”できる、って コトだよね！　あたしたち！
English: We could clean up! And be filthy rich!

Japanese: きゃっほう！
English: Woohoo!

Japanese: （‥‥あっというまに 元気になった‥‥）
English: (Wow, that was surprisingly easy...)

Japanese: もめんの布地に、きれいな 文字がビッシリ並んでいる。
English: A piece of cloth with a ton of finely-written characters jammed onto it.

Japanese: 霊媒師の心得みたいなものかな。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Probably esoteric knowledge only mediums would know. ...Hmm, let's see...

Japanese: タイトルは‥‥ “お金のたまる１０８の方法”
English: Here's the title in English... "108 Ways to Save Money".

Japanese: 前に見たときよりも、ちょっと 増えているような気がするな。
English: I'm pretty sure the number has gone up since the last time I saw it.

Japanese: あたしたち、いつだって 研究してるからね！
English: Well, we're always looking for new ways you know!

Japanese: ‥‥霊媒師の生活は、 やはりラクではないみたいだ。
English: Being a medium sounds like a rough way of life...

Japanese: たぶん‥‥倉院のツボが 置かれるはずだったんだろうね。
English: That's probably where the Sacred Urn was going to be displayed.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。
English: Yeah, I guess so...

Japanese: かわりに、ぼくの弁護士バッジ 貸してやろうか？
English: Should I put my attorney's badge there? You know, as a replacement?

Japanese: ううん‥‥ちょっと 小さすぎるんじゃないかな。
English: Hmm... I think it's a little too small for that.

Japanese: あたしの勾玉なんか どうかな。
English: What about my Magatama?

Japanese: ううん‥‥やっぱり 小さすぎるんじゃないかな。
English: Now you're being silly... That's definitely too small.

Japanese: 《倉院の呪詞》が書いてある ビョウブだよ。
English: It's the folding screen of Kurain Sacred Writings.

Japanese: あたしは読めないけどね。
English: But I can't read what it says on it.

Japanese: え！　大切な呪文じゃないの？
English: Really? But I thought it was some kind of important spell!

Japanese: ああ。あたし、おかあさんに 読んでもらっておぼえたから。
English: Ahh, yeah. My mother read it and taught me to memorize it.

Japanese: 忘れたらピンチだね。
English: I'll be in trouble if I ever forget it.

Japanese: （‥‥倉院流霊媒道も ガケっぷちに立たされてるな）
English: (Looks like the Kurain Channeling School is teetering dangerously on the edge...)

Japanese: いやー、よくできてるね。 あたしたちの里のミニチュア。
English: Wow, this is incredible! It's a tiny version of my whole village!

Japanese: あ、そうだ！　なるほどくんも、 事務所のミニチュアを作ろうよ！
English: Hey! I've got a great idea! We can make a miniature of your office, Nick!

Japanese: 事務所、どうでもいいガラクタが あふれかえっているからな。
English: I don't know about that. The whole place is overflowing with junk.

Japanese: カンゼンに再現しようとしたら、 おジイさんになっちまうよ。
English: You'd die before you finish painstakingly recreating that mess.

Japanese: その前に、お部屋を 片づければいいじゃない。
English: Well, we could always clean it up first.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: *shrug* True.

Japanese: 古そうなツボやら何やら。 ‥‥これも秘宝なの？
English: They're just a bunch of old urns... Are these really "treasures", too?

Japanese: そりゃそうだよ！ だって、“秘宝展”だからね！
English: Of course they are! This is a "Treasure Exhibit", isn't it!?

Japanese: ああ。ま、そりゃそうだね。
English: Well yeah, I guess so.

Japanese: （そういうものなのか？ “秘宝展”って‥‥）
English: (So all it takes to be a "treasure" is age? No wonder nobody likes museums...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 高菱屋　地下倉庫
English: October 13 Lordly Tailor Basement Warehouse

Japanese: あ‥‥な、成歩堂さん！
English: Oh...! It's you, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 霧緒さん‥‥ （まだ、オロオロしてるぞ）
English: Ms. Andrews... (What's she still hanging around down here for?)

Japanese: あの！　ど、どうですか？ 倉院のツボのほうは‥‥
English: Um, so! How is it going!? What about the Sacred Urn...?

Japanese: ツボ‥‥？　ああ、なんだ。 そっちはカタがつきそうですよ。
English: The urn...? Oh, that. It's been taken care of already.

Japanese: ナニよ、“ああ、なんだ”って！
English: What do you mean, "Oh, that"!?

Japanese: “カタがつきそう”‥‥？ もしかして、見つかったんですか！
English: "Taken care of"...? Do you mean... It's been found!?

Japanese: はい！　今日の法廷で 出てきたんですよ！
English: Yes! It was brought in during the trial today!

Japanese: えっ、ホントに！ さすが、成歩堂さん！
English: Wow! Really!? You really are the greatest, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: なるほどくんは カンケイないんです。
English: "Mr. Wright" had nothing to do with it.

Japanese: 見つけたの、仮面マスクの 奥さんでしたから！
English: It was Mask☆DeMasque's wife that found it!

Japanese: とにかく、私‥‥ホッとしました！
English: Well, anyway, I'm... I'm so relieved!

Japanese: この防犯カメラとパソコン、 アイガ探偵が仕掛けたんだよね。
English: This is the camera and computer that Detective Atmey set up, huh?

Japanese: そう言ってたね。
English: That's what he said.

Japanese: それなのに、自分で写真に 写っちゃうなんて。
English: And yet he still let it take a picture of him.

Japanese: アイガ探偵も、あんがい お人よしだよね！
English: I guess he's not as smart as he pretends to be!

Japanese: （カメラは高菱屋が用意したモノで 小細工はできなかった‥‥）
English: (The camera belonged to Lordly Tailor, so he knew he couldn't tamper with it...)

Japanese: （哀牙探偵‥‥仮面マスクはなぜ、 わざわざそんなコトを‥‥？）
English: (So then why would Atmey, I mean, Mask☆DeMasque, allow that to happen...?)

Japanese: 大きな看板の一部みたいだ。 《バー》って書いてある。
English: It looks like a part of a big signboard. All I can read on it is the "bur".

Japanese: あ、わかった！ “ハンバーグ”じゃないかな。
English: Aha! I've got it! Maybe it's supposed to say, "Hamburger"!

Japanese: こんなにデカく“ハンバーグ”って 書いて、どうするんだよ。
English: Why would anyone write "Hamburger" that big?

Japanese: “世界ハンバーグ祭り”とか。
English: Like, maybe for the "World Hamburger Festival" or something?

Japanese: ちがうと思うけどなあ。
English: I kinda doubt it.

Japanese: あ、わかった！ “ラーメン”じゃないかな！
English: Aha! I've got it! Maybe it said, "Spaghetti"!

Japanese: ‥‥さっきから 食べものばっかりだな。
English: ...Why are you talking about food? Oh, wait. That's normal for you.

Japanese: そもそも“ラー”じゃなくて “バー”だぞ！
English: Anyway, it says, "bur". There's no way it could be "Spaghetti".

Japanese: 書きまちがえた、とか。 “みそラーメン祭り”。‥‥どう？
English: Well, maybe it was a typo! It might have said, "Spaghetti Festival". What do you think?

Japanese: ‥‥楽しそうだとは思うよ。
English: OK to me... Besides, Spaghetti is the only thing more tangled than your reasoning.

Japanese: あ、ハシゴだ。
English: Look, a ladder!

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: That's a "step"-ladder.

Japanese: どうちがうの？ 同じようなものじゃない。
English: So? What's the difference?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ようよ、 なるほどくん。
English: You need to stop judging things based on narrow-minded cultural assumptions, Nick!

Japanese: ははは。
English: R-Right... sorry.

Japanese: （‥‥どこかで、同じような 会話をかわしたような‥‥）
English: (Why do I feel like we've had this exact same conversation before somewhere...?)

Japanese: アイガ探偵、これで後ろアタマを 怪人に殴られたって言ってたよね。
English: Detective Atmey said he got hit on the back of the head with this by the thief.

Japanese: まあ、もちろんウソだったけど。
English: But that was obviously a lie.

Japanese: あの探偵さんが怪人だった わけですからね‥‥。
English: Because it turned out that Atmey was the real thief... It's still hard to believe...

Japanese: （気にしてるみたいだな、 霧緒さん‥‥）
English: (It looks like Adrian feels really bad about it...)

Japanese: 私、セキニンを持って なおしますから、そのカタナ！
English: I'm going to take full responsibility and fix that sword myself!

Japanese: こう見えても、腕力には 自信があるんです！
English: I know I don't look it, but I have complete confidence that I can fix it right up!

Japanese: そんなカタナの１本や２本 ‥‥ヒン曲げてやりますとも！
English: I promise I'll fix it so you never knew it was bent in the first place!

Japanese: あ、あの。くれぐれも お手やわらかに‥‥。
English: P-Please be gentle with it...

Japanese: あそこに捨てられてる 粗末な木バコは、もしかして‥‥？
English: That pathetic-looking wooden box...

Japanese: あっ！　倉院のツボを しまっておいたハコだ！
English: Ahh!! That's the box that had the Sacred Urn in it!

Japanese: さわっちゃダメだよ。犯人の 手がかりが残ってるかもしれない。
English: Don't touch it! There may be clues to the thief's identity on it!

Japanese: ううう‥‥あたしが一生懸命 作ったのに‥‥
English: Grrr... I worked so hard to make that box!

Japanese: え！　真宵ちゃんが作ったの？ あの粗末な木バコ‥‥
English: Huh!? You MADE that poor excuse for a box, Maya?

Japanese: ソマツって言うな！
English: What's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: なんで捨てちゃったのかな、犯人。 自信作だったのに‥‥。
English: And I-I was so proud of it... *sniffle sniffle*

Japanese: （仮面マスク‥‥いっしょに 盗んであげればよかったのに）
English: (Poor Maya. Maybe it'd have been better if Mask☆DeMasque had taken the box, too...)

Japanese: ぼくたちが最初に見たときは、 こんなトコロにはなかったね。
English: The statue wasn't where it is now when we first came here, right?

Japanese: うん。たしかに、扉の すぐ横に立っていたよ。
English: Yeah, it was right next to the door, wasn't it?

Japanese: 動かしたとしたら‥‥ 仮面マスクだよな、たぶん。
English: Well, if someone moved it, it was probably Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: 倉庫に入るとき、視線を感じた のかな。供子さまの。
English: Maybe he didn't like being watched by Mystic Ami while he stole the urn.

Japanese: ‥‥コワいこと、言うなよ。
English: ...Hey! Cut it out! You're giving me the creeps with that kind of talk!

Japanese: 扉の向こうが倉庫に なっているんだ。
English: It looks like the door to the actual storeroom.

Japanese: いろんな大きさのハコが こっちから見えるよ。
English: I can see lots of big boxes and stuff back there.

Japanese: 倉院のツボ、あそこに しまってあったんだよね‥‥。
English: I guess that's where they were keeping the Sacred Urn.

Japanese: 入っちゃダメだぞ。
English: Remember, Maya. Ms. Andrews said we're not allowed in there.

Japanese: 《整理整とん》って書いてある。
English: It says, "ORGANI".

Japanese: ひらがなだから、 なるほどくんでも安心だね。
English: No it doesn't, Nick! It says, "ORGANIZE"! You just can't see the last two letters.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、そのワリに 散らかってるな。
English: Yeah, well I guess no one else could read it either, because this place is a mess.

Japanese: 《せいりせいとん》って 書くべきだったね。
English: Yeah. So you should feel right at home, right?

Japanese: 見た感じ、乾いてから 数日はたっているみたいだ。
English: It looks to me like it's been dry for several days.

Japanese: このペンキの跡も、 何かとナゾが多いよねー。
English: There's something suspicious about this paint mark.

Japanese: シミの左下が欠けてるし、 なぜかピンクだし。
English: The bottom left part of it is shaped oddly and it's shocking pink.

Japanese: ‥‥ペンキの色はこの際、 どうでもいいと思うけど。
English: ...I don't see how the color of the paint is in any way important.

Japanese: ‥‥さっき、私も ニュースで聞きました。
English: I just heard all about it on the news...

Japanese: あの探偵さんが‥‥ 怪人だったんですよね。
English: So that detective was actually the thief all along!

Japanese: 今のところ、 そんな状況みたいです。
English: It looks that way right now.

Japanese: 私‥‥わざわざ仮面マスクに 警備を依頼しちゃったんですね‥‥
English: It-It's my fault. I'm the one who ended up hiring DeMasque to guard the treasures...

Japanese: まあまあ。 そんなコトもありますよ。
English: Don't blame yourself. You were just doing your job.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 テキトーになぐさめない。
English: Hey, Nick, if she wants to apologize, you should let her.

Japanese: あの。アイガ探偵に警備を 依頼したのって、たしか‥‥？
English: So... When was it that you hired Detective Atmey again...?

Japanese: 今から、２０日ぐらい前です。
English: About 20 days ago.

Japanese: それで、仮面マスクの予告状が 届いたのが‥‥？
English: And when was it that Mask☆DeMasque's calling card arrived?

Japanese: １０日ほど前、だったと思います。
English: That was about 10 days ago.

Japanese: 自分が警備している高菱屋に 予告状を出したワケか‥‥。
English: So he sent a calling card to the very place he was hired to guard...

Japanese: やっぱり、アレだね。
English: I guess that's it then.

Japanese: アイガ探偵、どうしても ほしかったんだよ。倉院のツボが。
English: Detective Atmey must have really wanted the Sacred Urn after all.

Japanese: （‥‥そうなんだろうか‥‥）
English: (I guess so...)

Japanese: ええッ！　かか、仮面マスクが‥‥ あの、殺人を‥‥？
English: What!? So Mask☆DeMasque m-murdered someone as well...?

Japanese: 今のところ、 そんな状況みたいです。
English: Well, that's how things look right now.

Japanese: でも！　仮面マスクは、ここで ツボを盗んでいたんじゃあ‥‥？
English: Yes, but... I thought that he was here stealing the urn at the time...?

Japanese: まあまあ。相手は怪人ですから、 なんでもアリですよ。
English: Well... We're talking about a criminal mastermind, so anything is possible.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 テキトーなコト言わない。
English: Nick, let's get down to business already.

Japanese: あの。事件のあった夜ですけど‥‥ アイガ探偵、アヤシイところは？
English: On the night of the theft, did you notice anything suspicious about Detective Atmey?

Japanese: さあ‥‥わかりません。
English: No... I couldn't have.

Japanese: あの探偵さん、いつもかくれていて 姿を見せませんでしたから。
English: After all, he was hidden the entire time. I never even caught a glimpse of him.

Japanese: （それがアイツの手口だった、 ってワケか‥‥）
English: He claims that's the way he always operates...

Japanese: そうやってアリバイを作って、 怪人になって現れるんだね。
English: That's just what he says so he can have an alibi while he commits the thefts himself.

Japanese: コッソリ怪人の衣装に着替えて、 こうやって現場写真に写ったんだ。
English: Yeah, he was caught on the crime scene photo dressed up as Mask☆DeMasque pretty well.

Japanese: ホント、よかったですね。 ツボが戻ってきて‥‥
English: I'm so glad that you got your Sacred Urn back...

Japanese: ええ。ただ‥‥ちょっと、 気になるコトがあるんです。
English: Yes... But there's still something that bothers me about it.

Japanese: き、気になるコト？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: ハッキリしないんですけど‥‥
English: I'm not exactly sure...

Japanese: 戻ってきたツボ‥‥なんか、 感じが変わったような気がして‥‥
English: But somehow the urn that came back seems different...

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうなんですか？
English: R-Really...?

Japanese: 霧緒さん、何か知りませんか？ ツボのコト‥‥
English: You wouldn't know anything about that, would you Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: ししし、知りませんよ！ ももも、モチロン！
English: N-No, I don't know anything! Wh-Why would I?

Japanese: （サイコ・ロック‥‥！）
English: (A Psyche-Lock...!)

Japanese: ど、どういうこと？ なるほどくん！
English: What do you think this means, Nick!?

Japanese: ヘンテコなツボのヒミツ‥‥ そのカギをにぎっているのは、
English: It means the person that holds the secret to the mystery of the Sacred Urn...

Japanese: この霧緒さん、というコト らしいね‥‥
English: ...is our very own Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: 倉院のツボのこと‥‥ 何か、ご存じなんですね？
English: So... Do you know anything about the Sacred Urn?

Japanese: わ、私がですか‥‥？
English: Wh-Who? M-Me?

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、まれかさんが ツボを取り戻してくれたんです。
English: In court today, Ms. DeLite brought the urn back...

Japanese: でも‥‥なんか、あたしの知ってる ものと、ちょっとちがう気がして。
English: But... It's a little different from the urn I remember.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥霧緒さんは、 何か知っているんだ‥‥）
English: (So Adrian does know something that she's not telling us...)

Japanese: サツジンもいいけど、ツボのコトも よろしくね、なるほどくん。
English: Nick, I want to find out about the murder, but I also want to know about the urn.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥気になるな。 こっちも、カタをつけないと）
English: (I'd like to know myself. Another loose end to tie up, I guess.)

Japanese: そのバッジのおかげ‥‥ なんですよね。
English: I owe a great deal of thanks to that badge...

Japanese: ‥‥今、私がこうして がんばっていられるのも。
English: ...It's the only reason that I'm still here today.

Japanese: いや‥‥ちがいますよ。
English: Oh, come on now. That's not true...

Japanese: 『バッジじゃなくて、 このボクのおかげなんですよ！』
English: I think what Nick means to say is, "It wasn't thanks to the badge, it was thanks to ME!"

Japanese: ‥‥と、こう言いたいワケだよね、 なるほどくんとしては。
English: Tell the truth, Nick! I know that's what you were thinking!

Japanese: もっとちがうよ！
English: N-No way, Maya!

Japanese: 《倉院の里・秘宝展》は 私の最初のシゴトですから。
English: The Treasures of Kurain exhibit is my first job after last year's ordeal.

Japanese: なんとしても、 成功させたいんです！
English: And I really wanted it to be a success!

Japanese: せっかくだから、ポスターも 作り直したらどうですか？
English: I've got an idea! Why don't you redo the whole poster?

Japanese: “あの仮面マスクもおすすめ！” 《秘宝展》の上につけましょうよ！
English: You could put something like, "Voted Best Treasure Exhibit by Mask☆DeMasque"!

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さん！ それ、イタダキですっ！
English: ...Why Maya! That's a fantastic idea!

Japanese: やっぱり、光るモノがあったほうが 会場も華やかになりますから。
English: I thought the whole exhibit would be brightened up by something shiny and gold.

Japanese: ムリを言って、分家の霊場さんから お借りしましたの。
English: That's why I went to the trouble of borrowing it from a branch family training hall.

Japanese: カッコイイですよねー、黄金像。
English: A gold statue is totally cool in my book.

Japanese: ここに届くのがおくれちゃって。 展示できないかと思いましたけど。
English: It arrived rather late, so I thought I wouldn't be able to put it on display.

Japanese: ところで‥‥この像、事件当夜に 動かされたみたいなんですけど‥‥
English: By the way, it looks like this statue was moved on the night of the theft...

Japanese: 何か、ご存じないですか？
English: Do you know anything about that?

Japanese: え！　い、いえ！　私じゃ ありませんよ！　動かしたのは。
English: Huh! N-No! It-It certainly wasn't me that moved it!

Japanese: （‥‥何を あたふたしてるんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why is she so flustered all of a sudden...?)

Japanese: 予告状が届く前から、探偵さんに 警備を依頼したんですけど‥‥
English: I hired Detective Atmey for security before we even received a calling card...

Japanese: やっぱり、まちがいだったの でしょうか‥‥
English: I suppose that was a mistake...

Japanese: （哀牙探偵‥‥いや。 怪人☆仮面マスクは‥‥）
English: Atmey... I mean, Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: （自分が警備している秘宝展に、 自分で予告状を送りつけた‥‥）
English: He sent a calling card to the very exhibit he was hired to guard...

Japanese: やっぱり、盗みやすいから、 かなあ。
English: I wonder if it was because he knew it would be easy to steal.

Japanese: でも、ウタガイも かかりやすいと思いますけど‥‥。
English: Yes, but he should have also known that he could easily become a suspect.

Japanese: 防犯カメラは、高菱屋で 用意したものです。
English: The security camera was provided by Lordly Tailor Department Store.

Japanese: 盗難事件が起こった翌日にも キチンと調べましたから‥‥
English: I took a close look at it on the day after the theft...

Japanese: この写真と時刻だけは、 ホンモノと保証できますよ。
English: The timestamp on the photo is definitely real.

Japanese: すみません。今はまだ、 自分のコトでせいいっぱいで‥‥
English: I'm sorry I can't help more, but I'm so busy taking care of everything in front of me.

Japanese: 私、まだ《倉院の里・秘宝展》を あきらめたワケじゃないですから。
English: I still haven't given up on the Treasures of Kurain exhibit.

Japanese: 霧緒さん！　そのコトバを 待ってましたッ！
English: Oh, Ms. Andrews! I'm so happy to hear you say that!

Japanese: （秘宝展のことでアタマが いっぱいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like the Treasure Exhibit is the only thing she has on her mind...)

Japanese: 半年ぶりにお会いすると、 やはり見ちがえますね。
English: You've certainly changed since I last saw you 7 months ago.

Japanese: そ、そうですか？
English: R-Really?

Japanese: たくましくなったような 気がしますよ。
English: Yes, you look like you've bulked up a little.

Japanese: （そう言われてみると‥‥）
English: (Well, now that she mentions it...)

Japanese: （急にたくましく なったような気がしてきた）
English: (I guess I have gotten a little stronger...)

Japanese: ‥‥タンジュンだなあ、 なるほどくんは。
English: You're such a dork, Nick.

Japanese: 秘宝展では、真宵さんのコトも 紹介しようと思ってるんですよ。
English: Ms. Fey, I'd love to introduce you at the exhibit, if you don't mind.

Japanese: ホントですかっ！　あたし、 なんだって協力しちゃいますよ！
English: Really? I'd be happy to do whatever I can to help!

Japanese: あ！　なんだったら あたし、歌いましょうか？
English: Oh, I have an idea! Maybe I could sing a song?

Japanese: 《倉院音頭》とか《霊媒おけさ》 とか《マヨイ節》とか。
English: "The Master of Kurain" or "The Spirit Song" or perhaps "Maya's Theme"?

Japanese: え！　そんな持ち歌が あるんですか？
English: That sounds wonderful! I had no idea such songs actually existed.

Japanese: ありませんけど。 なるほどくんに作らせますから。
English: Well they don't... yet. I was going to make Nick write them.

Japanese: ‥‥ムリ言うなよ。
English: ...Be serious.

Japanese: かわいらしい子ですよね。 半年前、お世話になりました。
English: She's such a cute little girl. I'm deeply in her debt for what she did 7 months ago.

Japanese: はみちゃん、ていうんですよ。 霊媒師をめざして修行中です！
English: Her name is Pearl. She's training to be a spirit medium!

Japanese: 手に職をつけようなんて、 とてもエラいですね。
English: Training at such a young age... That's very impressive.

Japanese: キャリア・ウーマンどうし、 がんばりましょう！
English: Well, as a fellow career woman, I salute her!

Japanese: きゃりあ・うーまん‥‥
English: Career woman, huh...?

Japanese: （いちおう、そういうコトに なるのかな‥‥霊媒師も）
English: (I guess that's one way to look at spirit mediums... since they're all women...)

Japanese: やはり‥‥警察に 知らせればよかったんですよね。
English: Now I realize I should have called the police.

Japanese: 仮面マスクの予告状‥‥。
English: As soon as I got that calling card from Mask☆DeMasque, I mean.

Japanese: そうするべきだったかも しれませんね。
English: Yeah, I guess you should have.

Japanese: ま。イトノコさんのユカイな失敗談 が１つ、増えるだけですけど。
English: Well, now Detective Gumshoe has one more failure to add to his list.

Japanese: あら。ダメ刑事さんなんですか？
English: Oh? You mean he's not a very good detective?

Japanese: （この場にイトノコ刑事が いなくてよかったな‥‥）
English: (It's a good thing he's not here to hear us talking about him...)

Japanese: まさか‥‥探偵さんが 仮面マスクだった、なんて‥‥。
English: So Detective Atmey was actually Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: そうとう落ちこんでるよ。
English: She really seems depressed.

Japanese: しかたないだろうね。
English: I can't say I blame her.

Japanese: ‥‥わざわざ、怪人に警備を 依頼しちゃったんだから。
English: She's the one who hired the fox to guard the henhouse.

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、私って 人を見る目がないですよね。
English: ...I'm really terrible at judging people, I guess.

Japanese: 半年前の事件で、それを 思い知らされたのに‥‥
English: I should have learned my lesson after what happened 7 months ago...

Japanese: また今回も！　こんな 探偵さんに引っかかって‥‥
English: But I didn't! I got taken in by this... this... flimflam detective!

Japanese: まあまあ。あたしたちが いるじゃないですか！
English: Don't feel so bad. After all, we're here to help!

Japanese: ええ！　成歩堂さんは リッパな方ですよね！
English: Yes! Mr. Wright is a really wonderful man, isn't he?

Japanese: （人を見る目がない霧緒さんに いわれてもなあ‥‥）
English: That should've made me happy, but she just admitted she's terrible at judging people...

Japanese: またまた。‥‥ウレシイくせに！
English: Oh, come on... I can tell you're happy anyway.

Japanese: すみません‥‥ムカシほど カオが広くないものですから。
English: I'm sorry, I don't know as many people as I used to.

Japanese: 特に参考になりそうなコトは お話しできませんわ。
English: So there really isn't anything I can tell you about that person.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 高菱屋　地下倉庫
English: October 13 Lordly Tailor Basement Warehouse

Japanese: あら。いらっしゃい、 おじょうちゃん。
English: Oh, Pearl. How nice to see you.

Japanese: こっ、こんにちは！
English: H-Hello there!

Japanese: しばらく見なかったけど‥‥ どこへ行ってたのかしら？
English: I haven't seen you around lately. What have you been up to?

Japanese: あの、わたくし。 このツボを調べていました！
English: Well, actually... I was having this urn examined!

Japanese: ！　あ、あら。 そうだったの‥‥
English: O-Oh... I see.

Japanese: （あらためて、このツボを よーく見てみると‥‥）
English: (Maybe if we take another good look at this urn...)

Japanese: （この部屋に、そのナゾを解く 手がかりがありそうだな‥‥）
English: (...we can figure out the mystery of what actually happened here...)

Japanese: なるほどくん！　もう一度、 よーく調べてみようよ！
English: Nick! Let's look around one more time!

Japanese: 見た感じ、乾いてから 数日はたっているみたいだ。
English: It looks to me like it's been dry for several days.

Japanese: このペンキの跡も、 何かとナゾが多いよねー。
English: There's something suspicious about this paint mark.

Japanese: シミの左下が欠けてるし、 なぜかピンクだし。
English: The bottom left part of it is shaped oddly and it's shocking pink.

Japanese: この色‥‥ツボについている ペンキと同じです！
English: This is the same color as the paint splotches on the urn!

Japanese: （‥‥となると、 気になるのは‥‥）
English: (So then the question is...)

Japanese: （このシミが欠けている “原因”だな‥‥）
English: (What's the cause of the odd shape...?)

Japanese: よーく、探してみましょうよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Let's look around here carefully, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 見た感じ、乾いてから 数日はたっているみたいだ。
English: It looks to me like it's been dry for several days.

Japanese: このペンキの跡も、 何かとナゾが多いよねー。
English: There's something suspicious about this paint mark.

Japanese: シミの左下が欠けてるし、 なぜかピンクだし。
English: The bottom left part of it is shaped oddly and it's shocking pink.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　もしかして、 この欠けている部分は‥‥！
English: Mr. Nick! Could it be that this odd shape is...!?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。 きっと、コイツだね。
English: Yeah... I'm pretty sure this is it.

Japanese: あ、それ！ ツボをしまっておいたハコ‥‥
English: Ah! That's the box the Sacred Urn was in...!

Japanese: このハコにも、少し ペンキがついてるだろ？
English: If you look here, there's a little bit of paint on the box as well!

Japanese: きゃあ、ホントだ！ ほらほら。合わせる合わせる！
English: Wow, you're right! Look! It matches!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか？ なるほどくん。
English: What is it, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 思ったとおりだよ‥‥。 ピッタリだ！
English: This is all turning out exactly as I thought it would...!

Japanese: 《ペンキの跡》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Paint Marks added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
English: (I think it's all starting to become clear...)

Japanese: （そろそろ見えてきたな。 ツボのナゾのコタエが‥‥）
English: (We're that much closer to solving the mystery of what happened to the urn...)

Japanese: ‥‥このハコ‥‥ちょっと 気になるんだよな。
English: This box, there's something about it that's bothering me.

Japanese: それ、倉院のツボを しまっておいたハコだよ！
English: That's the box that the Sacred Urn was in!

Japanese: これにも、ピンクの ペンキがついていますね‥‥。
English: It looks like there's some pink paint on it, too...

Japanese: やっぱり、倉院のツボに ついてるのと、同じ色だよ！
English: And it's definitely the same color as the stuff on the urn!

Japanese: （このペンキがどこでついたか‥‥ わかるような気がするな）
English: I think I know how the paint got on it now...

Japanese: じゃあ、そこをもう一度 調べてみようよ、なるほどくん！
English: Alright! Let's investigate again, Nick!

Japanese: 《ツボが入っていたハコ》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Urn Box added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ごめんなさい‥‥。ツボを 割ってしまったのは、私です。
English: I'm so sorry... I was the one... The one who broke the urn.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、小学校の先生にでも なった気分だな）
English: (...Boy does this make me feel like some sort of evil school teacher.)

Japanese: 私‥‥サイテイの女ですね。 自分のシッパイをかくそうなんて。
English: I'm a terrible person! Not only did I break it, but I tried to hide what I did!

Japanese: そ、そんなコト‥‥ね、ねえ。 はみちゃん。
English: Well... That's not so hard to understand. Is it, Pearly?

Japanese: え、ええ！　もうそれは。 わたくし‥‥わかりますとも！
English: N-No, not at all! I... know just how she feels!

Japanese: あれは、２週間ほど前のことです。
English: It happened about 2 weeks ago.

Japanese: ポスター用の撮影が終わって、 ツボがこちらへ着いた日のこと‥‥
English: Just after the poster photo was taken, on the same day the urn arrived here...

Japanese: 倉庫にしまうつもりで、ハコを 持って、ここへ下りてきたとき‥‥
English: ...I thought I'd put it away down here for safe-keeping. I was carrying it in the box...

Japanese: 私‥‥ペンキのカンにつまずいて、 ハデにひっくり返してしまって。
English: ...when I tripped on a paint can and lost my balance!

Japanese: そのひょうしに、持っていた ハコを落としちゃったんです。
English: The box I was carrying crashed to the ground!

Japanese: ‥‥ガシャッ‥‥
‥‥とてもイヤな音がして‥‥ 私、血の気が引きました。
English: I heard a terrible noise and I thought my heart was going to stop...

Japanese: そして‥‥おそるおそる、 フタを開けた、その瞬間！
English: Fearing the worst, I opened the lid of the box and that's when it happened!

Japanese: ハコのスキ間から、ツボのカケラが ザラザラと‥‥ペンキの上に！
English: The broken pieces of the urn fell out of the box and landed right in the paint!

Japanese: 私‥‥私、もう‥‥
English: I... I... I was in shock...

Japanese: 絶叫しちゃいましたーーーっ！
English: And let out a huge scream!

Japanese: ‥‥うーん。 ありそうなハナシですよねー。
English: ...Hmm. I can totally see how that could've happened.

Japanese: （たしかに、真宵ちゃんなら やりそうだ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, as clumsy as she is, I'm sure Maya understands...)

Japanese: 倉院の里の、一番ダイジな 秘宝と聞いていたものですから‥‥
English: Well, I knew it was the most important treasure in all of Kurain Village...

Japanese: 私、必死になって修理しました。 さいわい、カケラは大きかったし。
English: ...so I tried as hard as I could to fix it. Fortunately, the shards were pretty big.

Japanese: そこで、どうして“子供”が “供子”になっちゃったんですか？
English: And that's when the "I AM" got changed to "AMI"?

Japanese: 私、もとの文字を 知らなかったんです。
English: I-I didn't know how it was originally written.

Japanese: ごくふつうに修理したら、 “供子”になったのですけど‥‥
English: But any sane person fixing it would have assumed it said, "AMI"...

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか。 ごくふつうに‥‥
English: A-Any sane person...? Really?

Japanese: （春美ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Oh, Pearls...)

Japanese: （‥‥図画工作は ニガテって言ってたからな‥‥）
English: (She said she wasn't very good at spelling...)

Japanese: ツボはコッソリ倉庫にしまって、 それからは、だれも見ていません。
English: Anyway, I put the urn into the storeroom and no one had seen it since then.

Japanese: 証拠品《倉院のツボ》の データを書きなおした。
English: Sacred Urn updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: でも‥‥あたしたちが最初に ここへ来たとき、
English: But... There's something I don't get...

Japanese: あのペンキの跡は、 ありませんでしたよね？
English: When we first came here, I didn't see any paint stains.

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。あまりに 見苦しかったものですから。
English: Well, that's because it was so ugly and embarrassing.

Japanese: あの黄金像を使って、 かくしておいたのです。
English: I used the golden statue to cover it.

Japanese: （“綾里　供子の像”‥‥か！）
English: (The Ami Fey statue... Aha!)

Japanese: ぼくたちが、初めてここへ 来たのは‥‥
English: The first time that we came down here...

Japanese: 倉院のツボが盗まれた夜だった。
English: It was on the night that the Sacred Urn was stolen.

Japanese: あのとき‥‥
English: But Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥‥倉庫のカベや床に、 ペンキの跡はありませんでした。
English: There were no paint marks on the walls or floor of the warehouse when we were here.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょうど、あの日の お昼ごろのコトだったんです。
English: Well, there's a good reason for that. On the day of the crime, around noon...

Japanese: 山奥の霊場から、あの黄金像が 倉庫に届いたのは‥‥。
English: ...that golden statue just happened to arrive from the mountain training hall.

Japanese: そ、それで‥‥？
English: A-And?

Japanese: あの黄金像‥‥ペンキの跡をかくす のに、ちょうどいい大きさでした。
English: I realized that the statue would be the perfect size for covering up the paint stains.

Japanese: だから‥‥私、あの場所へ 置いておいたんです。
English: That's why... I put it where you first saw it.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥ そりゃ、置きますよねえ。
English: I see... Now it makes perfect sense.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。わたくし、 少しおかしいと思います‥‥
English: Um, but there's still one thing I find strange...

Japanese: ？　何かしら、おじょうちゃん。
English: What is it, Pearl?

Japanese: ツボが盗まれた次の日、 また、ここへ来たのですけれど‥‥
English: The day after the urn was stolen, we came here again...

Japanese: そのときは、像は動いていて‥‥ ペンキの跡が見えました。
English: At that time, the statue had been moved and the paint was clearly visible.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥そうだった）
English: (She's right about that...)

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか？　霧緒さん。
English: Well, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: え。わ、私ですか！ ‥‥そんな、知りません！
English: Huh! What? I, err... I don't know anything about that!

Japanese: ペンキをかくすために置いたのに、 動かすわけ、ないじゃないですか。
English: I placed it there to cover the paint, so why would I move it again?

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥いったい、 だれがそんなことを‥‥？
English: Well then who was it? Who would have done it and why...?

Japanese: （事件の前日‥‥像はたしかに 扉の近くにあった。しかし！）
English: (On the day before the theft, the statue was definitely closer to the door!)

Japanese: （その翌日。像は動かされていた。 いったい、なぜ‥‥？）
English: (Then the next day, it was moved. But why...?)

Japanese: 《綾里　供子の黄金像》の データを書きなおした。
English: Ami Fey's Golden Statue updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: It looks like...

Japanese: 倉院のツボと、例の殺人事件‥‥ 何か関係があるみたいだな。
English: ...there's some connection between the Sacred Urn and the murder case.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうして？　なるほどくん！
English: Wh-Why? Why do you think so, Nick?

Japanese: あの晩‥‥ホンモノの怪人、 天杉　優作はＫＢ警備にいた。
English: Because that night... the real thief, Ron DeLite, was at KB Security.

Japanese: ‥‥それなら、なぜここに もう１人の仮面マスクが現れたか？
English: ...So then why did another Mask☆DeMasque show up here?

Japanese: いろいろなコトが‥‥ ある事実を指し示している。
English: A lot of different things are pointing to one undeniable fact.

Japanese: ‥‥ある事実‥‥？
English: One undeniable fact...?

Japanese: （‥‥明日。殺人事件の裁判‥‥）
English: (The murder trial is starting tomorrow...)

Japanese: （もう一度、あの怪人に登場して もらうことになりそうだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like that thief is going to be making another appearance...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ほら！　ちゃんと 見つかりましたよ、倉院のツボ。
English: See? We got our Sacred Urn back.

Japanese: ホント、よかったですね！
English: That's really wonderful.

Japanese: ただ‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: このツボには、まだ ナットクできない点があります。
English: We're not entirely convinced it's the same urn.

Japanese: ‥‥霧緒さん。 協力してもらいますよ。
English: Ms. Andrews, we want you to cooperate with us.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Nnngh...

Japanese: 霧緒さん。あそこのカベに ぶちまけられたペンキですけど‥‥
English: Ms. Andrews, about the paint that's all over the wall...

Japanese: ま、まあっ！ あんなところに、ペンキがッ！
English: Oh! Look at that! It looks like paint alright!

Japanese: わ、私‥‥今まで ゼンゼン気づきませんでしたわ！
English: Is-Isn't that funny!? I never noticed that before!

Japanese: ここまでシラジラしいムード、 あたしも初体験だよ。
English: That has to be the most pathetic acting I have ever seen in my life.

Japanese: わたくし、いたたまれない キモチでいっぱいです。
English: I'm beginning to get very frustrated.

Japanese: （早くラクにしてあげないとな）
English: (Uh oh. I'd better do something to calm them down, and fast!)

Japanese: ‥‥リッパなハコですよね！
English: What a magnificent box!

Japanese: ハコをホメても しょうがないでしょう。
English: You're not going to throw us off the trail with flattery.

Japanese: それより、このハコに ついているペンキですけど‥‥
English: I want to know about the paint on this box...

Japanese: さ、さ、さ、さあ。 私には、なんのことだかサッパリ。
English: Well I... I'm sure I don't know anything about that!

Japanese: （シラを切るのは ニガテみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Some people just have a natural gift for lying... Adrian's not one of them.)

Japanese: ～倉院のツボ～
English: -- The Sacred Urn --

Japanese: 霧緒さん。倉院のツボについて、 何か知ってるんですね？
English: Ms. Andrews, do you know anything about the Sacred Urn?

Japanese: な‥‥“何か”って、なんですか！ 私、セキニンシャとして‥‥
English: D-Do I know "anything"!? I'm in charge of the entire Treasure Exhibit!

Japanese: 今日、法廷で提出された 倉院のツボは‥‥
English: The urn that was submitted before the court today...

Japanese: ‥‥以前のものと アキラカにちがうんですよ。
English: ...It's obviously not the same urn as before!

Japanese: そ、それは！　ええと‥‥そうよ！
English: W-Well, that's... That's um... ...True!

Japanese: もしかしたら、そのツボ。 に、ニセモノじゃないかしら！
English: Maybe it isn't the same! It could be... It could be a fake!

Japanese: ‥‥ニセモノ‥‥？
English: ...A fake?

Japanese: だって、そう考えるほうが 自然でしょう！　それとも‥‥
English: You're the one who said it wasn't the same! So that's the most obvious explanation!

Japanese: 法廷で提出されたツボが ホンモノだ、という証拠でも‥‥？
English: Do you have any evidence the urn that was submitted at the trial was genuine?

Japanese: いかがですか？　霧緒さん。
English: Well, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: ほ、ホラ！　やっぱり、 ニセモノだったんですよ！
English: See, look! It was a fake after all!
Huh?

Japanese: だって‥‥成歩堂さんの 証拠も、ニセモノじゃないですか！
English: I mean, your evidence is fake too, as I expected!

Japanese: （どうやら、この証拠‥‥ イミがなかったみたいだな）
English: (Oops, I guess this isn't going to help me much...)

Japanese: ナットクしてもらえましたか？ ‥‥それとも‥‥
English: So have I convinced you? Or...

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥ このツボはホンモノです。
English: Sorry to break it to you, but the urn is the genuine article.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが倉院の里に 持ち帰って、調べてくれました。
English: Pearls went back to Kurain Village and had it examined.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。 それならいいんですけど‥‥。
English: Is-Is that right? That's nice, but I don't see how...

Japanese: どうやら、このツボ‥‥ また、割れたみたいですね。
English: What she discovered was that the vase was broken... again.
D-Did you say... "again"?

Japanese: １年前に、割れたことが あったんですよ。
English: Yes. It was broken once a year ago.

Japanese: そして、どうやら‥‥ 同じコトが起こった。
English: And now it looks like the same thing has happened.

Japanese: ‥‥しかも、最近。
English: And quite recently, too...

Japanese: さ‥‥最近！　そのツボが 割れた、と言うんですか？
English: R-Recently!? Are you saying that this urn was broken recently!?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes. That's exactly what I'm saying.

Japanese: どうして‥‥わかるんですか？ このツボが最近割れた、なんて！
English: Why do you think it was broken recently? How do you know?

Japanese: それで？　成歩堂さん。
English: So, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は、はい？
English: Huh?

Japanese: どうして、このツボが最近割れた ってわかるんですか？
English: Why do you think it was broken recently again?

Japanese: いや、だから、それは‥‥その。
English: Uh, well... B-Because... ...Umm, I just think so.

Japanese: うつむいていては、 わかりませんよ？
English: Sorry, but that's not a valid explanation.

Japanese: （追いつめるつもりが‥‥ 追いつめられちまった！）
English: (Grrr... The hunter has become the hunted!)

Japanese: と、とにかく！　割れたんですよ！ パリンと！　最近！
English: W-Well it was! It was broken recently! Come on! You know it!

Japanese: ‥‥それだけは、ゆずれません！
English: I'm not willing to yield to you on this point!

Japanese: や、ヤケにならないでください。 ‥‥もう一度、聞きましょうか？
English: G-Get a grip, please! ...Now, I'll ask you one more time, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: このポスター‥‥最近 作ったんですよね？
English: This poster... It was made recently, right?

Japanese: ポスター‥‥？ あっ！　こ、これは‥‥！
English: Poster...? Ah! The poster for the exhibit!

Japanese: ここに運ばれてくるまで、 ツボには“子供”と書かれていた。
English: At the time when this photo was taken, the urn said, "I AM" on it.

Japanese: しかし！　今はなぜか、 “供子”と書かれている！
English: But now, for some mysterious reason, it says, "AMI"!

Japanese: ‥‥ツボが割れて、修理するとき、 とりちがえてしまったからです！
English: When the urn was fixed, the letters were transposed!

Japanese: きゃあああっ！
English: AAAAAAH!

Japanese: こ、“子供”‥‥なんて。 私、知りませんでした‥‥
English: "I AM"...? What does that even mean? I don't know anything about that...!

Japanese: このポスターの撮影には 立ち会わなかったものですから。
English: I wasn't even there when the photo for this poster was taken.

Japanese: それは、シッパイでしたね。 じゃあ‥‥話してください。
English: That was a mistake. Now... Tell the truth.

Japanese: あ。ま、待ってください！
English: Ah! W-Wait!

Japanese: な、なんですか‥‥？
English: For?

Japanese: つ、ツボが割れたからと言って ‥‥私にはカンケイありません！
English: Ev-Even if the urn was broken, I had nothing to do with it!
...Huh?

Japanese: そ、そうです！　きっと、写真を 撮影したスタッフのだれかが！
English: Y-Yes, that's it! It must have been one of the people at the photo shoot!

Japanese: つるっと落としちゃったんです！ ‥‥そうに決まってますッ！
English: They probably dropped it! I'm sure that's what happened!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥まだ、 ロックは残っているからな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... It looks like I still haven't broken the entire Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: ツボが‥‥この高菱屋で割れた、 っていう証拠はあるんですか！
English: Do you have any proof the urn was broken here at Lordly Tailor?

Japanese: どうですか？　霧緒さん！
English: Well, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥やっぱり、 思ったとおりの方でした。
English: I knew I was right about you, Mr. Wright...
Excuse me?

Japanese: 最後のさいごで、 やさしいところを見せてくれる‥‥
English: You're a softie at heart, aren't you...?

Japanese: そういうところがあるんですよね。 ‥‥ホッとしました。
English: I knew you'd never hurt me on purpose. What a relief...

Japanese: （‥‥まちがえたらしいな、 どうも‥‥）
English: (Grrr, I guess that wasn't the right piece of evidence.)

Japanese: い、いえいえ！　今年の成歩堂は ヒトアジちがいますよ、霧緒さん！
English: I-I think you're mixing up the me of a year ago and the me of today, Ms. Andrews!

Japanese: でも‥‥それなら、 ちゃんと答えてください！
English: Well then... Prove me wrong by coming up with the right answer!

Japanese: どうですか？　霧緒さん。
English: Well, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: あの‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Umm... What is this supposed to mean?

Japanese: このツボには‥‥ ピンクのペンキがついてます。
English: This urn has these pretty little pink paint splotches on it.

Japanese: ううっ‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: そして‥‥ツボが入っていた、 このハコにも。
English: And there's some on the box the urn was stored in, too!

Japanese: ‥‥どう見ても、 同じペンキですよね。
English: No matter how you look at it, the paint seems to be exactly the same, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: だ、だから‥‥なんですか？
English: S-So? What does that prove?

Japanese: ツボは、ハコごと落とされた‥‥ そして、同時にペンキがついた。
English: That the urn was dropped along with the box! That's when they both got paint on them.

Japanese: ‥‥ここまでは、いいですか？
English: ...Are you with me so far?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: それなら、話はカンタンです。
English: The rest of the story is obvious.

Japanese: “このハコが落とされたのが、 ここ、高菱屋の地下倉庫である”
English: "This box was dropped right here in Lordly Tailor's basement warehouse."

Japanese: ‥‥それが証明できれば、 ツボもここで割れたことになる。
English: ...If I can prove that, it means that the urn was broken here, too.

Japanese: そんな証拠、あるんですか？
English: So can you prove that?

Japanese: そのハコが、この倉庫で落とされた っていう証拠が‥‥？
English: Can you prove that box was dropped here in the basement warehouse?

Japanese: どうですか！　霧緒さん！
English: Well, Ms. Andrews!?

Japanese: あの‥‥それは、こっちが 聞きたいです。
English: Um... Actually, I think I should be the one asking the questions.
Huh...?

Japanese: 最近どうですか？　成歩堂さん。
English: Are you feeling ill, Mr. Wright? Have you been getting enough sleep?

Japanese: はたらきすぎて、 つかれているのでは‥‥？
English: Are you sure you haven't been working yourself too hard?

Japanese: （追いつめるつもりが、 いたわられてしまった‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, she's turning this thing around on me now...)

Japanese: たまには、ゆっくり休んだ ほうがいいですよ。
English: You really should take some time off and try to relax.

Japanese: と、とりあえず、このモンダイを ハッキリさせてから休みます！
English: I-I can relax later, after I've solved this mystery!

Japanese: （コタエはハッキリ わかっているのに‥‥！）
English: (Argh! The answer is right here! I know it!)

Japanese: （もう少し、倉庫を調べてみた ほうがいいのかな‥‥？）
English: (Maybe I need to investigate the warehouse some more...?)

Japanese: ‥‥もう、わかってるんじゃ ないですか？　‥‥霧緒さん。
English: I think you already know where I'm going with this, don't you?

Japanese: ‥‥は‥‥、はい。 まあ、ウスウスは。
English: Y-Yes... More or less...

Japanese: 地下倉庫のカベと床にも、ハデに ペンキがブチまけられていた。
English: There's pink paint splattered on the floor and walls of this basement warehouse.

Japanese: あのペンキ‥‥ハジの部分が 少し、欠けていますね？
English: But there's an odd shape imprinted into a part of the paint stain, am I right?

Japanese: ええ‥‥
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: このハコをそこに当てると、 ペンキの跡とピッタリ合いました。
English: If you put this box into the impression in the paint, you can see it fits perfectly.

Japanese: つまり！　このハコは あそこで落とされた！
English: Which means this box was dropped right there!

Japanese: そして、そのとき‥‥中に 入っていたツボも、割れたんです！
English: And that... is when the urn was broken!

Japanese: さすが‥‥成歩堂さんッ！
English: Your name does you justice, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: どうですか？　霧緒さん。
English: Well, Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: あの‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Umm... What is this supposed to mean?

Japanese: このツボには‥‥ ピンクのペンキがついてます。
English: There is pink paint all over this urn.

Japanese: ううっ‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: 地下倉庫のカベと床にも、ハデに ペンキがブチまけられていた。
English: And there is pink paint all over the floor and walls of this basement warehouse.

Japanese: つまり‥‥このツボは、 あそこで割れたんです！
English: In other words... This urn was broken there!

Japanese: 今度こそ‥‥言い逃れは できませんよね。霧緒さん。
English: You can't weasel out of this one, Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: ‥‥で、でも！
English: B-But...!

Japanese: （うお。まだ 言い逃れるつもりかよ‥‥！）
English: (Uh-oh. She's trying to make her escape...!)

Japanese: ぴ、ピンクのペンキなんて、 どこにでもあるじゃないですか！
English: B-But you can get pink paint anywhere!

Japanese: ぼくの事務所にはありませんよ。
English: Well there's none in my office, that's for sure!

Japanese: わ、私のお部屋にはあります！
English: W-Well there is in my room!

Japanese: （ウソつけ！）
English: (Liar!)

Japanese: と、とにかく！
English: An-Anyway...!

Japanese: ツボについているペンキと、 床にこぼれているペンキ‥‥
English: The paint on the urn and the paint on the floor...

Japanese: 同じものだっていう証拠は あるんですか！
English: There's no proof that it's the same paint!

Japanese: （まったく‥‥ ムチャクチャを言うなあ）
English: (Come on! This is getting ridiculous!)

Japanese: このツボのペンキと、床のペンキを つなぐ証拠は‥‥
English: The proof linking the paint on the urn to the paint on the floor is...

Japanese: どうですか！　霧緒さん！
English: Well, Ms. Andrews!?

Japanese: あの‥‥それは、こっちが 聞きたいです。
English: Um... Actually, I think I should be the one asking the questions.
Huh...?

Japanese: 最近どうですか？　成歩堂さん。
English: Are you feeling ill, Mr. Wright? Have you been getting enough sleep?

Japanese: はたらきすぎて、 つかれているのでは‥‥？
English: Are you sure you haven't been working yourself too hard?

Japanese: （追いつめるつもりが、 いたわられてしまった‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, she's turning this thing around on me now...)

Japanese: たまには、ゆっくり休んだ ほうがいいですよ。
English: You really should take some time off and try to relax.

Japanese: と、とりあえず、このモンダイを ハッキリさせてから休みます！
English: I-I can relax later, after I've solved this mystery!

Japanese: （コタエはハッキリ わかっているのに‥‥！）
English: (Argh! The answer is right here! I know it!)

Japanese: （もう少し、倉庫を調べてみた ほうがいいのかな‥‥？）
English: (Maybe I need to investigate the warehouse some more...?)

Japanese: このハコ‥‥ ツボが入っていたものですね？
English: This box... The urn was stored in this, right?

Japanese: ええ‥‥そうですけど。
English: Y-Yes... That's right.

Japanese: この箱にも‥‥ピンクの ペンキがついてます。
English: Well, there's pink paint on this box as well.

Japanese: どうやら、わかったみたいですね。
English: I think you already know where I'm going with this, don't you?

Japanese: ‥‥は‥‥、はい。 まあ、ウスウスは。
English: Y-Yes... More or less...

Japanese: 床のペンキ‥‥ハジの部分が 少し、欠けていますね？
English: The paint on the floor has an odd shape imprinted in it, doesn't it?

Japanese: ええ‥‥
English: Y-Yes...
